Expected circled items: Jelibon, Kraker, Yulaf Ezmesi, Avokado (4 items at Leo's threshold). Cannot verify from extracted text.

items.WS4_B2: MCR en düşük olan eşik değeri
items.WS4_B3: Eşik 10'da MCR 0.20, eşik 8'de MCR 0.30 — 10 daha iyi.

item_schema.WS4_visual_task: Circle the 4 misclassified items at Leo's original threshold position on the data table. Expected: Jelibon, Kraker, Yulaf Ezmesi, Avokado.
item_schema.WS4_B1: Best threshold value (numeric) — expected value from answer key: 8 or 10 depending on dataset version
item_schema.WS4_B2: Criterion used to select best threshold — must reference MCR or accuracy metric
item_schema.WS4_B3: Comparison statement — must cite at least two MCR values with their thresholds
item_schema.WS4_B4: MCR calculation — formula + arithmetic. Expected: MCR = (FP+FN)/total, with correct substituted values.
item_schema.WS4_B5: Evaluate Pia's claim: 'The best threshold is the one with fewest mistakes' — must state correct/incorrect AND give reasoning

raw_ocr.WS4_B2: MCR en düşük olan eşik değeri
raw_ocr.WS4_B3: Eşik 10'da MCR 0.20, eşik 8'de MCR 0.30 — 10 daha iyi.
raw_ocr.WS4_B5: Pia'nın ifadesi doğru; en düşük MCR en az hata demektir.
raw_ocr.ws_snapshot: Sample_Student en iyi eşik değerini doğru belirlemiş ve MCR hesabını adım adım göstermiş. Pia'nın önerme değerlendirmesi de yerinde. Hesap doğru.

arithmetic_check.note: Total = 20 is plausible if dataset has 20 items. FP=2, FN=2 → MCR=4/20=0.20. Internally consistent.

warnings: WS4_visual_task: Circle task not captured in text extraction — cannot verify whether student correctly identified the 4 misclassified items.
WS4_B4: Total denominator = 20 (implies 20-item dataset). Cross-check against WS5 which uses 10-item dataset — possible version discrepancy.

student_snapshot.llm_observations: All text items complete. Student correctly identifies best threshold using MCR criterion, provides comparison with competing threshold, executes correct formula. Pia evaluation is accurate with reasoning.

WS4_visual_task.rationale: Circle task requires visual inspection of student's physical worksheet. Cannot be extracted from text. Re-check of scanned image required.

WS4_B1.rationale: Student selected threshold=10 as best. At şeker threshold=10, MCR=0.20 (confirmed by B4 arithmetic). This is a valid best threshold for the sugar feature. Answer key accepts 8 or 10 if MCR values match.

WS4_B2.rationale: Criterion is explicitly metric-based: 'MCR en düşük olan eşik değeri'. This correctly states that the best threshold minimizes MCR. No aesthetic or intuitive reasoning.
WS4_B2.evidence_quote: MCR en düşük olan eşik değeri

WS4_B3.rationale: Comparison cites both MCR values with their thresholds: eşik 10 → MCR 0.20, eşik 8 → MCR 0.30. Two-threshold comparison is correct and sufficient.
WS4_B3.evidence_quote: Eşik 10'da MCR 0.20, eşik 8'de MCR 0.30 — 10 daha iyi.

WS4_B4.rationale: Formula written correctly: MCR = (FP + FN) / toplam. Values substituted: (2+2)/20 = 0.20. Arithmetic is correct. Step-by-step shown.

WS4_B5.rationale: Student correctly evaluates Pia's claim as true AND provides reasoning: 'en düşük MCR en az hata demektir'. This links the metric (MCR) to its real-world meaning (fewer errors). Reasoning is present, not just agreement.
WS4_B5.evidence_quote: Pia'nın ifadesi doğru; en düşük MCR en az hata demektir.

summary.scoring_note: All 5 scored items full credit. Visual circle task not captured but does not affect text-based scoring. Student demonstrates: (1) metric-based threshold selection, (2) MCR formula application with correct arithmetic, (3) correct evaluation of a peer claim with reasoning.

Acquire_confirmed: WS4_B4: MCR formula applied correctly — LO3.1.1 (knows evaluation metric)
Deepen_confirmed: WS4_B2: Threshold selection criterion is metric-based, not intuitive — LO3.2.1 (comparing models using data-driven criterion)
WS4_B3: Compared two thresholds using MCR values — LO3.2.1
WS4_B5: Correctly evaluated a peer's claim and linked MCR to its meaning — LO3.2.2 (interpreting model evaluation)

indicators.LO3.2.1: confirmed — metric-based comparison of two thresholds
indicators.LO3.2.2: confirmed — MCR interpretation linked to classification error rate

gate_4_aicft.ws_contribution_to_portfolio: WS4 is the strongest single Deepen signal. Student moves from 'what is MCR' (WS1) to 'use MCR to choose between models' (WS4). This is the core Deepen competency: using evaluation metrics to compare alternatives rather than guessing.
